commitMessage: Recolor fruit for last 3 fruits of an Iron Snake level
summary: The developer implemented a visual countdown cue for Iron Snake Mode by recoloring the fruit for the last 3 fruits of each level (violet → magenta → white), including a DRY refactor extracting a shared ironSnakeRemaining() helper and committing the result.

steps.description: Add IRON_SNAKE_FINAL_FRUIT_COLORS constant next to the HUNGER_* palette at main.ts:37-39
steps.note: The constant was added at line 40 (immediately after ironSnakeGoalForLevel), using a Record<number, [string, string, string]> keyed by fruits-remaining. Three distinct hues — violet, magenta, white — were chosen over a blend or single warning color because they give an unambiguous per-fruit countdown; the progression to white on the final fruit was specifically chosen to feel climactic against the dark isometric board.

steps.description: Extract ironSnakeRemaining() helper and replace duplicated combined/remaining math in updateScore()
steps.note: The private ironSnakeRemaining() method was added near hungerFactor() and the duplicated inline math in updateScore() was replaced with a single call, eliminating the DRY violation. The helper correctly handles two-player mode via the existing isTwoSnakeMode() check and returns 0 in classic mode, making it safe as a fallback guard for the color lookup.

steps.description: Recolor the fruit in draw() by computing foodColors near the hunger-tint block and using it in the case 'food': branch
steps.note: The implementation mirrors the existing hunger-tint pattern exactly — computing a triad before the render switch and passing it into the pre-existing drawBlock call — avoiding any new rendering infrastructure. The ?? red fallback covers classic mode (ironSnakeRemaining() returns 0) and early-level states, and the menu-demo.ts decorative fruit was deliberately left untouched since it has no level concept.

steps.description: Optional: add a line to the README feature list for Iron Snake Mode noting the final-fruit color cue
